top_categories.workout_context: 206 miles. Your legs will beg to quit after 120. This trains the power output you need from mile 130 onward.
Race-pace efforts that mimic STP — Seattle to Portland's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before July.
206 miles rewards fat oxidation. This volume work shifts your fuel mix so you're still burning fat at hour 5, not bonking at hour 3.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 206 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 5,100ft of climbing on flat to gently rolling paved roads does to your power file on race day.
STP — Seattle to Portland's flat to gently rolling paved roads forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 206 miles.
5,100ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
Tempo is STP — Seattle to Portland's race pace. The wattage you'll hold for 206 miles on flat to gently rolling paved roads. Train it until it's automatic.
STP — Seattle to Portland's flat to gently rolling paved roads rewards steady threshold power. Surging and recovering costs more watts than just holding.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of STP — Seattle to Portland. Train it until it feels boring.
At STP — Seattle to Portland, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on flat to gently rolling paved roads. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.

race_overlay.nutrition: Ultra-distance fueling for 206 miles: 80–100g carbs/hour from mile 1 — don’t wait until you’re hungry. Practice your exact race-day nutrition in every long training ride. Carry backup calories. 206 miles burns 8,000–12,000+ calories — you cannot replace them all, but you must try.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, Race Simulation, and HVLI Extended to prepare you for 206 miles of flat to gently rolling paved roads in Seattle to Portland, Washington/Oregon, USA.
